Replication Material

Coordination inside government administrations: Lessons from the EU Commission

doi: https://doi.org/10.1111/gove.12525

Roman Senninger, Daniel Finke, and Jens Blom-Hansen

Contact: rsenninger@ps.au.dk

Data

Please visit https://dataverse.harvard.edu/dataverse/R_Senninger to download the data used in this manual.

Load Required Packages

library("visreg")
library("circlize")
library("gplots")
library("heatmaply")

Content

This manual provides code to reproduce our results. We start with the results in Figure 1 showing a chord diagram of coordination relationships between DGs.

Figure 1

load("./chord.RData")

col_fun <- colorRamp2(range(chord$sum), c("gray90", "gray50"), transparency = 0.5)
grid.col = c("grey80")
par(cex = 0.7)
chordDiagram(chord, grid.col = grid.col, col = col_fun, directional = 1)

#Figure 2

load("./efcosine.RData")

heatmap.2(efcosine,  
          col = Greys,
          cexCol = 0.8,
          cexRow = 0.8,
          srtCol=45, 
          scale="none",
          margins=c(3,0), # ("margin.Y", "margin.X")
          trace='none', 
          symkey=FALSE, 
          symbreaks=FALSE, 
          dendrogram='none',
          density.info='histogram', 
          denscol="grey40",
          keysize=1, 
          #( "bottom.margin", "left.margin", "top.margin", "left.margin" )
          key.par=list(mar=c(4,0,3,0)),
          # lmat -- added 2 lattice sections (5 and 6) for padding
          lmat=rbind(c(5, 4, 2), c(6, 1, 3)), lhei=c(2.5, 5), lwid=c(1, 10, 1))

Table 1

Reproduce regression results in Table 1:

load("./cisnet.RData")

model1 <- glm(coordination ~ cosine1 + spatial_lag + as.factor(year) + as.factor(DG), family = binomial(link = "logit"),  data = cisnet)

model2 <- glm(coordination ~ cosine1 * Salience + spatial_lag + as.factor(year) + as.factor(DG), family = binomial(link = "logit"),  data = cisnet)

Figure 3

# left panel 

visreg(model1, "cosine1", scale = "response", xlab="policy overlap", ylab = "P(coordination)", rug = 1, main = "", alpha = 0.01, 
       line=list(col="black", lwd=0.5),
       fill=list(col="gray80"), ylim=c(0, 1))

# right panel 

visreg(model2, "cosine1", by = "Salience", scale = "response", rug = 1, xlab = "policy overlap", ylab = "P(coordination)", overlay=T, 
       ylim=c(0, 1), line.par=list(col = c("black", "black"), lwd=0.5, lty = c("solid", "dashed")), fill.par=list(col=c("gray90", "gray70")))

Additional Material

The replication material includes the term frequency-inverse document frequency matrix as described on p. 11 of the article.

load("./tfidf.RData
